  "customSqlDownload.help.whatIsThis": "This download format allows you to aggregate occurrences by their taxonomic, temporal and/or spatial properties. For example, a data cube can be configured to aggregate occurrences by family, month and grid cell of the European Environment Agency reference grid (three dimensions) and count the number of occurrences (a measure) per combination. The result is a CSV file.\n\nOnce configured, a SQL query will be created to generate the data cube. For more advanced use, it is possible to further customize the requested download by editing the SQL query.",
  "customSqlDownload.help.dimensions": "A dimension represents an aspect along which data can be aggregated. Selecting a higher resolution (e.g., species over family, date over year, 100 m over 10 km) will result in more categories and therefore more records.",
  "customSqlDownload.help.taxonomicDimension": "This dimension aggregates occurrences by their taxonomic rank.",
  "customSqlDownload.help.temporalDimension": "This dimension aggregates occurrences by time.",
  "customSqlDownload.help.dataQuality": "Additional quality filters to apply to the data.",
  "customSqlDownload.help.grid": "This dimension aggregates occurrences in a spatial grid.",
  "customSqlDownload.help.gridResolution": "The size of each grid cell.",
  "customSqlDownload.help.randomizePoints": "For occurrence records with a coordinate uncertainty that covers more than one grid cell, should a random cell be chosen? If not, the cell containing the centroid of the record is used.",
  "customSqlDownload.help.measurements": "A calculated quantitative value for each combination of dimensions.",
  "customSqlDownload.help.occurrenceCount": "The number of occurrences.",
  "customSqlDownload.help.higherTaxonomy": "Additional higher taxonomic ranks for which the number of occurrences should also be included. Useful to assert sampling bias.",
  "customSqlDownload.help.minCoordinateUncertainty": "The lowest recorded coordinate uncertainty (in meters). Useful to assert the spatial precision of the data.",
  "customSqlDownload.help.minTemporalUncertainty": "The lowest recorded temporal uncertainty (in seconds). Useful to assert the temporal precision of the data.",
  "customSqlDownload.help.editSql": "The easiest way to download and explore data is via the occurrence search user interface. But for complex queries and aggregations, the SQL editor provides more freedom.",

  "trends.theseChartsIllustrate1": "These charts illustrate the change in availability of the species occurrence records over time.",
  "trends.noOccRecords": "Number of occurrence records",
  "trends.occByKingdomChartText": "The number of available records categorized by kingdom. \"Unknown\" includes records with taxonomic information that cannot be linked to available taxonomic checklists.",
  "trends.recordsByKingdom": "Records by kingdom",
  "trends.animaliaByBasisOfRecordText": "The number of animal records categorized by the basis of record. \"Unknown\" includes records without defined basis of record or with an unrecognized value for basis of record.",
  "trends.recordsForAnimalia": "Records for Animalia",
  "trends.plantaeByBasisOfRecordText": "The number of plant records categorized by the basis of record. \"Unknown\" includes records without defined basis of record or with an unrecognized value for basis of record.",
  "trends.recordsForPlantae": "Records for Plantae",
  "trends.theseChartsIllustrate2": "These charts illustrate the change in the number of species for which occurrence records are available.",
  "trends.speciesCounts": "Species counts",
  "trends.speciesCountDescription": "Species counts are based on the number of binomial scientific names for which GBIF has received data records, organized as far as possible using synonyms recorded in key databases such as the Catalogue of Life. Since many names are not yet included in these databases, some proportion of these names will be unrecognized synonyms and do not represent valid species. Therefore these counts can be used as an indication of richness only, and do not represent true species counts. All data have been processed using the same, most recent version of the common GBIF backbone taxonomy, and comparisons over time are therefore realistic.",
  "trends.definition": "Definition",
  "trends.numOccSpeciesByKingdom": "The number of species with available occurrence records, categorized by kingdom.",
  "trends.speciesCountByKingdom": "Species count by kingdom",
  "trends.numSpeciesFromSpecimens": "The number of species associated with specimen records.",
  "trends.speciesCountsForSpecimenRecords": "Species count for specimen records",
  "trends.speciesCountForObservationRecords": "Species count for observation records",
  "trends.numSpeciesAssociatedWithObsRecords": "The number of species associated with observation records.",
  "trends.timeAndSeasonality": "Time and seasonality",
  "trends.theseChartsIllustrate3": "These charts illustrate changes in the spread of records by year of occurrence and by day of year, indicating the extent of possible bias towards more recent periods or particular seasons. Snapshots are provided for approximately 3-year intervals to show changes in spread.",
  "trends.speciesCountsAreBasedOnTheNumberOfBinomialScientificNames": "Species counts are based on the number of binomial scientific names for which GBIF has received data records, organized as far as possible using synonyms recorded in key databases such as the Catalogue of Life. Since many names are not yet included in these databases, some proportion of these names will be unrecognized synonyms and do not represent valid species. Therefore these counts can be used as an indication of richness only, and do not represent true species counts. All data have been processed using the same, most recent, version of the common GBIF backbone taxonomy, and comparisons over time are therefore realistic.",
  "trends.recordsByYearOfOccurrence": "Records by year of occurrence",
  "trends.theNumberOfOccurrenceRecordsAvailable": "The number of occurrence records available for each year since 1950.",
  "trends.speciesByYearOfOccurrence": "Species by year of occurrence",
  "trends.theNumberOfSpeciesForWhichRecordsAreAvailable": "The number of species (see above) for which records are available for each year since 1950.",
  "trends.recordsByDayOfYear": "Records by day of year",
  "trends.recordsAvailableForEachDayOfTheYear": "The number of occurrence records available for each day of the year.",
  "trends.speciesByDayOfYear": "Species by day of year",
  "trends.speciesForWhichRecordsAreAvailableForEachDayOfTheYear": "The number of species (see above) for which records are available for each day of the year.",
  "trends.note": "Note",
  "trends.theseChartsMayRevealPatternsThatRepresentBiasesInDataCollection": "These charts may reveal patterns that represent biases in data collection (seasonality, public holidays) or potential issues in data management (disproportionate numbers of records shown for the first or last days in the year or each month or week). Such issues may arise at various stages in data processing and require further investigation.",
  "trends.byGeneratingTheseChartsAnIssueWasDetected": "By generating these charts [an issue](https://github.com/gbif/gbif-api/issues/2) was detected in the GBIF processing which contributes to the spike seen on the first day of the year in several charts and will be addressed.",
  "trends.completenessAndPrecision": "Completeness and precision",
  "trends.theseChartsIllustrate4": "These charts illustrate changes in the completeness (see below) of available records and in the precision of these records with respect to time, geography and taxonomy.",
  "trends.aRecordIsHereDefinedToBeCompleteIfItIncludes": "A record is here defined to be complete if it includes an identification at least to species rank, valid coordinates, a full date of occurrence and a given basis of record (e.g. Observation, specimen etc).",
  "trends.completeness": "Completeness",
  "trends.theseChartsIllustrate5": "These charts illustrate changes in the number of records considered complete according to the definition above. Separate charts separately show the same information for specimen records and for observation records. Subsequent charts illustrate the component elements that affect the number of complete records.",
  "trends.allRecords": "All records",
  "trends.specimenRecords": "Specimen records",
  "trends.observationRecords": "Observation records",
  "trends.taxonomicPrecision": "Taxonomic precision",
  "trends.theseChartsIllustrate6": "These charts illustrate changes in the number of available records which include an identification at least to the species rank. The numbers of records identified to an infraspecific rank or to a genus are also shown.",
  "trends.geographicPrecision": "Geographic precision",
  "trends.theseChartsIllustrate7": "These charts illustrate changes in the number of available records which include coordinates for which no known issues have been detected. For records without accepted valid coordinates, these charts also show the number of records for which the country of occurrence is known.",
  "trends.temporalPrecision": "Temporal precision",
  "trends.theseChartsIllustrate8": "These charts illustrate changes in the number of available records which include a complete date including year, month and day. The numbers of records including only the month and year or only the year are also shown.",
  "trends.geographicCoverageForRecordedSpecies": "Geographic coverage for recorded species",
  "trends.theseChartsIllustrate9": "These charts illustrate change in the number of species for which occurrence records are available and the geographic coverage of records for each species at different scales. In each chart, species are classified by the number of cells at a given scale for which occurrence records for the species are available. More cells indicates greater data coverage for the species. Naturally, some species are only known from a small area, so low cell counts do not necessarily indicate lack of relevant data.",

  "directory.title": "Directory",
  "directory.group.executiveCommittee.title": "Executive Committee",
  "directory.group.executiveCommittee.description": "The Executive Committee operates on behalf of the Governing Board when the Board is not in session and within the areas of responsibility delegated to it by the Governing Board. Among its functions are monitoring of the performance of the Secretariat in carrying out the decisions taken by the Governing Board including implementation of the Strategic Plan and the Work Programme, and management of the budget.",
  "directory.group.scienceCommittee.title": "Science Committee",
  "directory.group.scienceCommittee.description": "The GBIF Science Committee is an advisory committee that oversees the development and progress of the GBIF work programme and makes recommendations to the Governing Board, the Executive Committee and the Secretariat. You can read its terms of reference [here.](/document/80877/current-terms-of-reference-for-the-science-committee)",
  "directory.group.budgetCommittee.title": "Budget committee",
  "directory.group.budgetCommittee.description": "The Budget Committee is an advisory committee dealing with various financial issues relating to the administration of GBIF funds. Among other functions, it oversees the audit of the annual accounts submitted to the Governing Board, and it provides guidance for the selection of GBIF’s auditing company. The committee makes recommendations to the Governing Board, the Executive Committee and the Secretariat. Its terms of reference can be found [here.](/document/80669/terms-of-reference-for-the-gbif-budget-committee)",
  "directory.group.nsg.title": "Nodes Steering Group",
  "directory.group.nsg.description": "Established in 2011, the Nodes Steering Group (NSG) consists of the chair and vice-chairs of the [Participant Node Managers Committee](/contact-us/directory?group=nodesCommittee) joined by a regional representative from each of GBIF’s six regions ([Africa](/the-gbif-network/africa), [Asia](/the-gbif-network/asia), [Europe and Central Asia](/the-gbif-network/europe), [Latin America/Caribbean](/the-gbif-network/latin-america), [North America](/the-gbif-network/north-america) and [Oceania](/the-gbif-network/oceania)). The NSG’s objectives are to formulate specific recommendations to relevant GBIF bodies based on feedback provided by Participant nodes, and to provide advice on GBIF’s annual work programmes as they relate to the efforts of GBIF nodes.",
  "directory.group.nodesCommittee.title": "Node Managers Committee",
  "directory.group.nodesCommittee.description": "The Participant Node Managers Committee (or Nodes Committee) is a dynamic group formed by the node managers appointed by all Participants (countries and international organizations). The committee includes three Chair positions established through voting by the Governing Board for a maximum of two two-year terms. You can read its terms of reference [here](/document/80571/terms-of-reference-for-the-participant-node-managers-committee-nodes).",
  "directory.group.secretariat.title": "GBIF Secretariat",
  "directory.group.secretariat.description": "The GBIF Secretariat, located at the Natural History Museum in Copenhagen, Denmark, is charged with developing, executing and reporting on the GBIF work programme. The tasks of the Secretariat include:\n - operating the GBIF portal and associated informatics\n - coordinating activities of the network related to data mobilization, publishing and management\n - monitoring data gaps and use of GBIF-mediated data for science, and\n - operating central communication and collaboration services",
  "directory.group.voting.title": "Voting participants",
  "directory.group.voting.description": "Countries that have signed the GBIF Memorandum of Understanding and agree to make a basic financial contribution to GBIF core funds.",
  "directory.group.associateCountries.title": "Associate country participants",
  "directory.group.associateCountries.description": "Countries that have signed the GBIF Memorandum of Understanding but do not make a financial contribution to GBIF core funds, and do not have a vote on the GBIF Governing Board.",
  "directory.group.associateParticipants.title": "Other associate participants",
  "directory.group.associateParticipants.description": "Intergovernmental organizations, international organizations and other organizations with an international scope, and economies, that have signed the GBIF Memorandum of Understanding.",

  "enums.occurrenceIssueDescription.ZERO_COORDINATE": "Coordinates are exactly 0/0, often indicating an actual null coordinate.",
  "enums.occurrenceIssueDescription.COORDINATE_OUT_OF_RANGE": "The supplied coordinates lie outside of the range for decimal lat/lon values (-90/90, -180/180).",
  "enums.occurrenceIssueDescription.COORDINATE_INVALID": "A coordinate value is given in some form, but GBIF is unable to interpret it. Possible reasons include, i.a., coordinates that fall out of range(larger/lower than 90/-90 or 180/-180, depending) or text values that cannot be interpreted.",
  "enums.occurrenceIssueDescription.COORDINATE_ROUNDED": "In the data interpretation the original coordinates are rounded to 6 decimals (~1m precision).",
  "enums.occurrenceIssueDescription.GEODETIC_DATUM_INVALID": "The geodetic datum could not be interpreted, because the supplied term cannot be matched against the vocabulary of known values.",
  "enums.occurrenceIssueDescription.GEODETIC_DATUM_ASSUMED_WGS84": "If the datum is null, data interpretation assumes the record coordinates are in WGS84.",
  "enums.occurrenceIssueDescription.COORDINATE_REPROJECTED": "The original coordinates were successfully reprojected from a different geodetic datum to WGS84.",
  "enums.occurrenceIssueDescription.COORDINATE_REPROJECTION_FAILED": "The given decimal latitude and longitude could not be reprojected to WGS84 based on the provided datum.",
  "enums.occurrenceIssueDescription.COORDINATE_REPROJECTION_SUSPICIOUS": "Indicates successful coordinate reprojection according to provided datum, but which results in a datum shift larger than 0.1 decimal degrees.",
  "enums.occurrenceIssueDescription.COORDINATE_ACCURACY_INVALID": "Coordinate accuracy invalid",
  "enums.occurrenceIssueDescription.COORDINATE_PRECISION_INVALID": "Indicates an invalid or very unlikely coordinates precision. The value is not a decimal number as expected, or it has an unusually low or high for a margin of uncertainty.",
  "enums.occurrenceIssueDescription.COORDINATE_UNCERTAINTY_METERS_INVALID": "The value given for Coordinate uncertainty in meters, indicating the radius of uncertainty around the given decimal coordinates, is not a valid number, or lies outside a plausible range.",
  "enums.occurrenceIssueDescription.COORDINATE_PRECISION_UNCERTAINTY_MISMATCH": "Coordinate precision uncertainty mismatch",
  "enums.occurrenceIssueDescription.FOOTPRINT_SRS_INVALID": "The spatial reference system could not be interpreted, because the supplied term could not be matched against the vocabulary of known values.",
  "enums.occurrenceIssueDescription.FOOTPRINT_WKT_MISMATCH": "The footprint well-known text (point, line or polygon) conflicts with the given coordinates.",
  "enums.occurrenceIssueDescription.FOOTPRINT_WKT_INVALID": "The footprint well-known text (point, line or polygon) could not be interpreted.",
  "enums.occurrenceIssueDescription.COUNTRY_COORDINATE_MISMATCH": "The interpreted occurrence coordinates fall outside of the indicated country.",
  "enums.occurrenceIssueDescription.COUNTRY_MISMATCH": "Interpreted Country and Country code contradict each other.",
  "enums.occurrenceIssueDescription.COUNTRY_INVALID": "The country or countryCode given cannot be matched to the vocabulary for country names.",
  "enums.occurrenceIssueDescription.COUNTRY_DERIVED_FROM_COORDINATES": "If the country and country code are not supplied or cannot be matched to known values, data interpretation derives their content from the decimal coordinates through a lookup service.",
  "enums.occurrenceIssueDescription.CONTINENT_COORDINATE_MISMATCH": "The interpreted occurrence coordinates fall outside of the indicated continent.",
  "enums.occurrenceIssueDescription.CONTINENT_COUNTRY_MISMATCH": "The interpreted continent and country do not match up.",
  "enums.occurrenceIssueDescription.CONTINENT_INVALID": "The continent given cannot be matched to the vocabulary for continent names.",
  "enums.occurrenceIssueDescription.CONTINENT_DERIVED_FROM_COUNTRY": "The continent is based on the provided country, not the provided continent.",
  "enums.occurrenceIssueDescription.CONTINENT_DERIVED_FROM_COORDINATES": "If no value is supplied for the continent or if the values cannot be matched against a known vocabulary, data interpretation derives the continent from the decimal coordinates.",
  "enums.occurrenceIssueDescription.PRESUMED_SWAPPED_COORDINATE": "Coordinates seem to be swapped when testing against the interpreted country.",
  "enums.occurrenceIssueDescription.PRESUMED_NEGATED_LONGITUDE": "The supplied longitude value places the coordinates outside of the indicated country. Negating the longitude value would result in a country match.",
  "enums.occurrenceIssueDescription.PRESUMED_NEGATED_LATITUDE": "The supplied latitude value places the coordinates outside of the indicated country. Negating the latitude value would result in a country match.",
  "enums.occurrenceIssueDescription.RECORDED_DATE_MISMATCH": "The recording date specified as the eventDate string and the individual year, month, day are contradicting.",
  "enums.occurrenceIssueDescription.RECORDED_DATE_INVALID": "The recording date given cannot be intrepreted because is invalid. Reasons include: A non-existing date (e.g \"1995-04-34\"), Missing date parts (e.g. Event date without year), The date format does not follow the ISO 8601 standard (YYYY-MM-DD).",
  "enums.occurrenceIssueDescription.RECORDED_DATE_UNLIKELY": "The recording date is highly unlikely, falling either into the future or representing a very old date before 1600 that predates modern taxonomy.",
  "enums.occurrenceIssueDescription.TAXON_MATCH_FUZZY": "Matching to the taxonomic backbone can only be done using a fuzzy, non exact match.",
  "enums.occurrenceIssueDescription.TAXON_MATCH_HIGHERRANK": "The record can be matched to the GBIF taxonomic backbone at a higher rank, but not with the scientific name given. Reasons include: The name is new, and not available in the taxonomic datasets yet, The name is missing in the backbone's taxonomic sources for others reasons, Formatting or spelling of the scientific name caused interpretation errors.",
  "enums.occurrenceIssueDescription.TAXON_MATCH_AGGREGATE": "Matching to the taxonomic backbone can only be done on a species level, but the occurrence was considered a broader species aggregate or complex.",
  "enums.occurrenceIssueDescription.TAXON_MATCH_NONE": "Matching to the taxonomic backbone cannot be done cause there was no match at all or several matches with too little information to keep them apart(homonyms).",
  "enums.occurrenceIssueDescription.DEPTH_NOT_METRIC": "Set if supplied depth is not given in the metric system, for example using feet instead of meters.",
  "enums.occurrenceIssueDescription.DEPTH_UNLIKELY": "The values for minimum and maximum depth are negative or higher than 11000 (Mariana Trench depth in meters).",
  "enums.occurrenceIssueDescription.DEPTH_MIN_MAX_SWAPPED": "The values for minimum and maximum depth appear to the swapped.",
  "enums.occurrenceIssueDescription.DEPTH_NON_NUMERIC": "The values for minimum and maximum depth are non-numeric values and cannot be interpreted.",
  "enums.occurrenceIssueDescription.ELEVATION_UNLIKELY": "The values for minimum and maximum elevation are above the troposphere (17000 m) or below Mariana Trench (11000 m).",
  "enums.occurrenceIssueDescription.ELEVATION_MIN_MAX_SWAPPED": "The values for minimum and maximum elevation appear to the swapped.",
  "enums.occurrenceIssueDescription.ELEVATION_NOT_METRIC": "Set if supplied elevation is not given in the metric system, for example using feet instead of meters.",
  "enums.occurrenceIssueDescription.ELEVATION_NON_NUMERIC": "The values for minimum and maximum elevation are non-numeric values and cannot be interpreted.",
  "enums.occurrenceIssueDescription.MODIFIED_DATE_INVALID": "A (partial) invalid modified date is given. Reasons include: A non-existing date (e.g \"1995-04-34\"), Missing date parts (e.g. without year), The date format does not follow the ISO 8601 standard (YYYY-MM-DD).",
  "enums.occurrenceIssueDescription.MODIFIED_DATE_UNLIKELY": "The modified date given is in the future or predates unix time (1970).",
  "enums.occurrenceIssueDescription.IDENTIFIED_DATE_UNLIKELY": "The identification date is in the future or before Linnean times (1700).",
  "enums.occurrenceIssueDescription.IDENTIFIED_DATE_INVALID": "The identification date given cannot be intrepreted because is invalid. Reasons include: A non-existing date (e.g \"1995-04-34\"), Missing date parts (e.g. without year), The date format does not follow the ISO 8601 standard (YYYY-MM-DD).",
  "enums.occurrenceIssueDescription.BASIS_OF_RECORD_INVALID": "The given basis of record is impossible to interpret or very different from the recommended vocabulary: https://rs.gbif.org/vocabulary/dwc/basis_of_record.",
  "enums.occurrenceIssueDescription.TYPE_STATUS_INVALID": "The given type status is impossible to interpret or very different from the recommended vocabulary: https://registry.gbif.org/vocabulary/TypeStatus.",
  "enums.occurrenceIssueDescription.MULTIMEDIA_DATE_INVALID": "The creation date given cannot be intrepreted because is invalid. Reasons include: A non-existing date (e.g \"1995-04-34\"), Missing date parts (e.g. Event date without year), The date format does not follow the ISO 8601 standard (YYYY-MM-DD).",
  "enums.occurrenceIssueDescription.MULTIMEDIA_URI_INVALID": "The multimedia URL cannot be resolved, and may be malformed or contain invalid characters.",
  "enums.occurrenceIssueDescription.REFERENCES_URI_INVALID": "The references URL cannot be resolved, and may be malformed or contain invalid characters.",
  "enums.occurrenceIssueDescription.INTERPRETATION_ERROR": "An error occurred during interpretation, leaving the record interpretation incomplete.",
  "enums.occurrenceIssueDescription.INDIVIDUAL_COUNT_INVALID": "Individual count value not parsable into a positive integer.",
  "enums.occurrenceIssueDescription.INDIVIDUAL_COUNT_CONFLICTS_WITH_OCCURRENCE_STATUS": "The values given for the individual count and for the status of the occurrence (present/absent) contradict each other (e.g. the count is 0 but the status says \"present\").",
  "enums.occurrenceIssueDescription.OCCURRENCE_STATUS_UNPARSABLE": "The given occurrenceStatus value cannot be interpreted; it does not match any of the known (vocabulary) values that indicate the presence or absence of a species at or observation event.",
  "enums.occurrenceIssueDescription.OCCURRENCE_STATUS_INFERRED_FROM_INDIVIDUAL_COUNT": "The present/absent status of the occurrence was inferred from the individual count value because no status value was supplied explicitly. An individual count of 0 is interpreted as status=\"absent\", a value > 0 as \"present\".",
  "enums.occurrenceIssueDescription.OCCURRENCE_STATUS_INFERRED_FROM_BASIS_OF_RECORD": "The present/absent status of the occurrence was inferred from the basis of record value because no status value was supplied explicitly.",
  "enums.occurrenceIssueDescription.GEOREFERENCED_DATE_UNLIKELY": "The georeference date given is in the future or before Linnean times (1700).",
  "enums.occurrenceIssueDescription.GEOREFERENCED_DATE_INVALID": "The georeference date given cannot be interpreted because it is invalid. Reasons include: A non-existing date (e.g \"1995-04-34\"), Missing date parts (e.g. without year), The date format does not follow the ISO 8601 standard (YYYY-MM-DD).",
  "enums.occurrenceIssueDescription.AMBIGUOUS_INSTITUTION": "Multiple institutions were found in GRSciColl with the same level of confidence and it can't be determined which one should be accepted. For example, there are several institutions with the same code and country. See this FAQ on how to avoid ambiguous matches.",
  "enums.occurrenceIssueDescription.AMBIGUOUS_COLLECTION": "Multiple collections were found in GRSciColl with the same level of confidence and it can't be determined which one should be accepted. For example, there are several collections belonging to the same institution with the same code. See this FAQ on how to avoid ambiguous matches.",
  "enums.occurrenceIssueDescription.INSTITUTION_MATCH_NONE": "No match was found in GRSciColl. Either the entry doesn't exists in GRSciColl or it has a different code. Check GRSciColl and request update if needed.",
  "enums.occurrenceIssueDescription.COLLECTION_MATCH_NONE": "No match was found in GRSciColl. Either the entry doesn't exists in GRSciColl or it has a different code. Check GRSciColl and request update if needed.",
  "enums.occurrenceIssueDescription.INSTITUTION_MATCH_FUZZY": "A match was found in GRSciColl but it was matched fuzzily. To know more about why this has happened, you can use the lookup API to see the \"reasons\" returned in the response. A common case is when the name is used instead of the code or the identifier. To avoid fuzzy matches, publishers should use identifiers in additon to codes. More details available in this FAQ.",
  "enums.occurrenceIssueDescription.COLLECTION_MATCH_FUZZY": "A match was found in GRSciColl but it was matched fuzzily. To know more about why this has happened you can use the lookup API to see the \"reasons\" returned in the response. A common case is when the name is used instead of the code or the identifier. To avoid fuzzy matches, publishers should use identifiers in additon to codes. More details available in this FAQ.",
  "enums.occurrenceIssueDescription.INSTITUTION_COLLECTION_MISMATCH": "At least one possible collection match was found in GRSciColl but none of them belong to the institution matched.",
  "enums.occurrenceIssueDescription.POSSIBLY_ON_LOAN": "Possibly on loan",
  "enums.occurrenceIssueDescription.DIFFERENT_OWNER_INSTITUTION": "The institution doesn't match the owner institution.",
  "enums.occurrenceIssueDescription.TAXON_MATCH_SCIENTIFIC_NAME_ID_IGNORED": "The scientificNameID was not used when mapping the record to the GBIF backbone. This may indicate one of: The ID uses a pattern not configured for use by GBIF, The ID did not uniquely identify a concept in the checklist, The ID found a concept in the checklist that did not map to the backbone, A different ID was used, or the record names were used, as no ID lookup successfully linked to the backbone.",
  "enums.occurrenceIssueDescription.TAXON_MATCH_TAXON_CONCEPT_ID_IGNORED": "taxonConceptID was not used when mapping the record to the GBIF backbone. This may indicate one of: The ID uses a pattern not configured for use by GBIF, The ID did not uniquely identify a concept in the checklist, The ID found a concept in the checklist that did not map to the backbone, A different ID was used, or the record names were used, as no ID lookup successfully linked to the backbone.",
  "enums.occurrenceIssueDescription.TAXON_MATCH_TAXON_ID_IGNORED": "The taxonID was not used when mapping the record to the GBIF backbone. This may indicate one of: The ID uses a pattern not configured for use by GBIF, The ID did not uniquely identify a concept in the checklist, The ID found a concept in the checklist that did not map to the backbone, A different ID was used, or the record names were used, as no ID lookup successfully linked to the backbone.",
  "enums.occurrenceIssueDescription.SCIENTIFIC_NAME_ID_NOT_FOUND": "The scientificNameID matched a known pattern, but it was not found in the associated checklist. The backbone lookup was performed using either the names or a different ID field from the record. This may indicate a poorly formatted identifier or may be caused by a newly created ID that isn't yet known in the version of the published checklist.",
  "enums.occurrenceIssueDescription.TAXON_CONCEPT_ID_NOT_FOUND": "The taxonConceptID matched a known pattern, but it was not found in the associated checklist. The backbone lookup was performed using either the names or a different ID field from the record. This may indicate a poorly formatted identifier or may be caused by a newly created ID that isn't yet known in the version of the published checklist.",
  "enums.occurrenceIssueDescription.TAXON_ID_NOT_FOUND": "The taxonID found matched a known pattern, but it was not found in the associated checklist. The backbone lookup was performed using either the names or a different ID field from the record. This may indicate a poorly formatted identifier or may be caused by a newly created ID that isn't yet known in the version of the published checklist.",
  "enums.occurrenceIssueDescription.SCIENTIFIC_NAME_AND_ID_INCONSISTENT": "The scientificName provided in the occurrence record does not precisely match the name in the registered checklist when using the scientificNameID, taxonID or taxonConceptID to look it up. Publishers are advised to check the IDs are correct, or update the formatting of the names on their records.",
  "enums.occurrenceIssueDescription.TAXON_MATCH_NAME_AND_ID_AMBIGUOUS": "The GBIF Backbone concept was found using the scientificNameID, taxonID or taxonConceptID, but it differs from what would have been found if the classification names on the record were used. This may indicate a gap in the GBIF backbone, a poor mapping between the checklist and the backbone, or a mismatch between the classification names and the declared IDs (scientificNameID or taxonConceptID) on the occurrence record itself.",
  "enums.eventIssueDescription.ZERO_COORDINATE": "Coordinates are exactly 0/0, often indicating an actual null coordinate.",
  "enums.eventIssueDescription.COORDINATE_OUT_OF_RANGE": "The supplied coordinates lie outside of the range for decimal lat/lon values (-90/90, -180/180).",
  "enums.eventIssueDescription.COORDINATE_INVALID": "A coordinate value is given in some form, but GBIF is unable to interpret it. Possible reasons include, i.a., coordinates that fall out of range(larger/lower than 90/-90 or 180/-180, depending) or text values that cannot be interpreted.",
  "enums.eventIssueDescription.COORDINATE_ROUNDED": "In the data interpretation the original coordinates are rounded to 6 decimals (~1m precision).",
  "enums.eventIssueDescription.GEODETIC_DATUM_INVALID": "The geodetic datum could not be interpreted, because the supplied term cannot be matched against the vocabulary of known values.",
  "enums.eventIssueDescription.GEODETIC_DATUM_ASSUMED_WGS84": "If the datum is null, data interpretation assumes the record coordinates are in WGS84.",
  "enums.eventIssueDescription.COORDINATE_REPROJECTED": "The original coordinates were successfully reprojected from a different geodetic datum to WGS84.",
  "enums.eventIssueDescription.COORDINATE_REPROJECTION_FAILED": "The given decimal latitude and longitude could not be reprojected to WGS84 based on the provided datum.",
  "enums.eventIssueDescription.COORDINATE_REPROJECTION_SUSPICIOUS": "Indicates successful coordinate reprojection according to provided datum, but which results in a datum shift larger than 0.1 decimal degrees.",
  "enums.eventIssueDescription.COORDINATE_ACCURACY_INVALID": "Coordinate accuracy invalid",
  "enums.eventIssueDescription.COORDINATE_PRECISION_INVALID": "Indicates an invalid or very unlikely coordinates precision. The value is not a decimal number as expected, or it has an unusually low or high for a margin of uncertainty.",
  "enums.eventIssueDescription.COORDINATE_UNCERTAINTY_METERS_INVALID": "The value given for Coordinate uncertainty in meters, indicating the radius of uncertainty around the given decimal coordinates, is not a valid number, or lies outside a plausible range.",
  "enums.eventIssueDescription.COORDINATE_PRECISION_UNCERTAINTY_MISMATCH": "Coordinate accuracy invalid",
  "enums.eventIssueDescription.FOOTPRINT_SRS_INVALID": "The spatial reference system could not be interpreted, because the supplied term could not be matched against the vocabulary of known values.",
  "enums.eventIssueDescription.FOOTPRINT_WKT_MISMATCH": "The footprint well-known text (point, line or polygon) conflicts with the given coordinates.",
  "enums.eventIssueDescription.FOOTPRINT_WKT_INVALID": "The footprint well-known text (point, line or polygon) could not be interpreted.",
  "enums.eventIssueDescription.COUNTRY_COORDINATE_MISMATCH": "The interpreted occurrence coordinates fall outside of the indicated country.",
  "enums.eventIssueDescription.COUNTRY_MISMATCH": "Interpreted Country and Country code contradict each other.",
  "enums.eventIssueDescription.COUNTRY_INVALID": "The country or countryCode given cannot be matched to the vocabulary for country names.",
  "enums.eventIssueDescription.COUNTRY_DERIVED_FROM_COORDINATES": "If the country and country code are not supplied or cannot be matched to known values, data interpretation derives their content from the decimal coordinates through a lookup service.",
  "enums.eventIssueDescription.CONTINENT_COORDINATE_MISMATCH": "The interpreted occurrence coordinates fall outside of the indicated continent.",
  "enums.eventIssueDescription.CONTINENT_COUNTRY_MISMATCH": "The interpreted continent and country do not match up.",
  "enums.eventIssueDescription.CONTINENT_INVALID": "The continent given cannot be matched to the vocabulary for continent names.",
  "enums.eventIssueDescription.CONTINENT_DERIVED_FROM_COUNTRY": "The continent is based on the provided country, not the provided continent.",
  "enums.eventIssueDescription.CONTINENT_DERIVED_FROM_COORDINATES": "If no value is supplied for the continent or if the values cannot be matched against a known vocabulary, data interpretation derives the continent from the decimal coordinates.",
  "enums.eventIssueDescription.PRESUMED_SWAPPED_COORDINATE": "Coordinates seem to be swapped when testing against the interpreted country.",
  "enums.eventIssueDescription.PRESUMED_NEGATED_LONGITUDE": "The supplied longitude value places the coordinates outside of the indicated country. Negating the longitude value would result in a country match.",
  "enums.eventIssueDescription.PRESUMED_NEGATED_LATITUDE": "The supplied latitude value places the coordinates outside of the indicated country. Negating the latitude value would result in a country match.",
  "enums.eventIssueDescription.RECORDED_DATE_MISMATCH": "The recording date specified as the eventDate string and the individual year, month, day are contradicting.",
  "enums.eventIssueDescription.RECORDED_DATE_INVALID": "The recording date given cannot be intrepreted because is invalid. Reasons include: A non-existing date (e.g \"1995-04-34\"), Missing date parts (e.g. Event date without year), The date format does not follow the ISO 8601 standard (YYYY-MM-DD).",
  "enums.eventIssueDescription.RECORDED_DATE_UNLIKELY": "The recording date is highly unlikely, falling either into the future or representing a very old date before 1600 that predates modern taxonomy.",
  "enums.eventIssueDescription.TAXON_MATCH_FUZZY": "Matching to the taxonomic backbone can only be done using a fuzzy, non exact match.",
  "enums.eventIssueDescription.TAXON_MATCH_HIGHERRANK": "The record can be matched to the GBIF taxonomic backbone at a higher rank, but not with the scientific name given. Reasons include: The name is new, and not available in the taxonomic datasets yet, The name is missing in the backbone's taxonomic sources for others reasons, Formatting or spelling of the scientific name caused interpretation errors.",
  "enums.eventIssueDescription.TAXON_MATCH_AGGREGATE": "Matching to the taxonomic backbone can only be done on a species level, but the occurrence was considered a broader species aggregate or complex.",
  "enums.eventIssueDescription.TAXON_MATCH_NONE": "Matching to the taxonomic backbone cannot be done cause there was no match at all or several matches with too little information to keep them apart(homonyms).",
  "enums.eventIssueDescription.DEPTH_NOT_METRIC": "Set if supplied depth is not given in the metric system, for example using feet instead of meters.",
  "enums.eventIssueDescription.DEPTH_UNLIKELY": "The values for minimum and maximum depth are negative or higher than 11000 (Mariana Trench depth in meters).",
  "enums.eventIssueDescription.DEPTH_MIN_MAX_SWAPPED": "The values for minimum and maximum depth appear to the swapped.",
  "enums.eventIssueDescription.DEPTH_NON_NUMERIC": "The values for minimum and maximum depth are non-numeric values and cannot be interpreted.",
  "enums.eventIssueDescription.ELEVATION_UNLIKELY": "The values for minimum and maximum elevation are above the troposphere (17000 m) or below Mariana Trench (11000 m).",
  "enums.eventIssueDescription.ELEVATION_MIN_MAX_SWAPPED": "The values for minimum and maximum elevation appear to the swapped.",
  "enums.eventIssueDescription.ELEVATION_NOT_METRIC": "Set if supplied elevation is not given in the metric system, for example using feet instead of meters.",
  "enums.eventIssueDescription.ELEVATION_NON_NUMERIC": "The values for minimum and maximum elevation are non-numeric values and cannot be interpreted.",
  "enums.eventIssueDescription.MODIFIED_DATE_INVALID": "A (partial) invalid modified date is given. Reasons include: A non-existing date (e.g \"1995-04-34\"), Missing date parts (e.g. without year), The date format does not follow the ISO 8601 standard (YYYY-MM-DD).",
  "enums.eventIssueDescription.MODIFIED_DATE_UNLIKELY": "The modified date given is in the future or predates unix time (1970).",
  "enums.eventIssueDescription.IDENTIFIED_DATE_UNLIKELY": "The identification date is in the future or before Linnean times (1700).",
  "enums.eventIssueDescription.IDENTIFIED_DATE_INVALID": "The identification date given cannot be intrepreted because is invalid. Reasons include: A non-existing date (e.g \"1995-04-34\"), Missing date parts (e.g. without year), The date format does not follow the ISO 8601 standard (YYYY-MM-DD).",
  "enums.eventIssueDescription.BASIS_OF_RECORD_INVALID": "The given basis of record is impossible to interpret or very different from the recommended vocabulary: https://rs.gbif.org/vocabulary/dwc/basis_of_record.",
  "enums.eventIssueDescription.TYPE_STATUS_INVALID": "The given type status is impossible to interpret or very different from the recommended vocabulary: https://registry.gbif.org/vocabulary/TypeStatus.",
  "enums.eventIssueDescription.MULTIMEDIA_DATE_INVALID": "The creation date given cannot be intrepreted because is invalid. Reasons include: A non-existing date (e.g \"1995-04-34\"), Missing date parts (e.g. Event date without year), The date format does not follow the ISO 8601 standard (YYYY-MM-DD).",
  "enums.eventIssueDescription.MULTIMEDIA_URI_INVALID": "The multimedia URL cannot be resolved, and may be malformed or contain invalid characters.",
  "enums.eventIssueDescription.REFERENCES_URI_INVALID": "The references URL cannot be resolved, and may be malformed or contain invalid characters.",
  "enums.eventIssueDescription.INTERPRETATION_ERROR": "An error occurred during interpretation, leaving the record interpretation incomplete.",
  "enums.eventIssueDescription.INDIVIDUAL_COUNT_INVALID": "Individual count value not parsable into a positive integer.",
  "enums.eventIssueDescription.INDIVIDUAL_COUNT_CONFLICTS_WITH_OCCURRENCE_STATUS": "The values given for the individual count and for the status of the occurrence (present/absent) contradict each other (e.g. the count is 0 but the status says \"present\").",
  "enums.eventIssueDescription.OCCURRENCE_STATUS_UNPARSABLE": "The given occurrenceStatus value cannot be interpreted; it does not match any of the known (vocabulary) values that indicate the presence or absence of a species at or observation event.",
  "enums.eventIssueDescription.OCCURRENCE_STATUS_INFERRED_FROM_INDIVIDUAL_COUNT": "The present/absent status of the occurrence was inferred from the individual count value because no status value was supplied explicitly. An individual count of 0 is interpreted as status=\"absent\", a value > 0 as \"present\".",
  "enums.eventIssueDescription.OCCURRENCE_STATUS_INFERRED_FROM_BASIS_OF_RECORD": "The present/absent status of the occurrence was inferred from the basis of record value because no status value was supplied explicitly.",
  "enums.eventIssueDescription.GEOREFERENCED_DATE_UNLIKELY": "The georeference date given is in the future or before Linnean times (1700).",
  "enums.eventIssueDescription.GEOREFERENCED_DATE_INVALID": "The georeference date given cannot be interpreted because it is invalid. Reasons include: A non-existing date (e.g \"1995-04-34\"), Missing date parts (e.g. without year), The date format does not follow the ISO 8601 standard (YYYY-MM-DD).",
  "enums.eventIssueDescription.AMBIGUOUS_INSTITUTION": "Multiple institutions were found in GRSciColl with the same level of confidence and it can't be determined which one should be accepted. For example, there are several institutions with the same code and country. See this FAQ on how to avoid ambiguous matches.",
  "enums.eventIssueDescription.AMBIGUOUS_COLLECTION": "Multiple collections were found in GRSciColl with the same level of confidence and it can't be determined which one should be accepted. For example, there are several collections belonging to the same institution with the same code. See this FAQ on how to avoid ambiguous matches.",
  "enums.eventIssueDescription.INSTITUTION_MATCH_NONE": "No match was found in GRSciColl. Either the entry doesn't exists in GRSciColl or it has a different code. Check GRSciColl and request update if needed.",
  "enums.eventIssueDescription.COLLECTION_MATCH_NONE": "No match was found in GRSciColl. Either the entry doesn't exists in GRSciColl or it has a different code. Check GRSciColl and request update if needed.",
  "enums.eventIssueDescription.INSTITUTION_MATCH_FUZZY": "A match was found in GRSciColl but it was matched fuzzily. To know more about why this has happened you can use the lookup API to see see the \"reasons\" returned in the response. A common case is when the name is used instead of the code or the identifier. To avoid fuzzy matches, publishers should use identifiers in additon to codes. More details available in this FAQ.",
  "enums.eventIssueDescription.COLLECTION_MATCH_FUZZY": "A match was found in GRSciColl but it was matched fuzzily. To know more about why this has happened you can use the lookup API to see the \"reasons\" returned in the response. A common case is when the name is used instead of the code or the identifier. To avoid fuzzy matches, publishers should use identifiers in additon to codes. More details available in this FAQ.",
  "enums.eventIssueDescription.INSTITUTION_COLLECTION_MISMATCH": "At least one possible collection match was found in GRSciColl but none of them belong to the institution matched.",
  "enums.eventIssueDescription.POSSIBLY_ON_LOAN": "Coordinate accuracy invalid",
  "enums.eventIssueDescription.DIFFERENT_OWNER_INSTITUTION": "The institution doesn't match the owner institution.",
  "enums.eventIssueDescription.TAXON_MATCH_SCIENTIFIC_NAME_ID_IGNORED": "The scientificNameID was not used when mapping the record to the GBIF backbone. This may indicate one of: The ID uses a pattern not configured for use by GBIF, The ID did not uniquely identify a concept in the checklist, The ID found a concept in the checklist that did not map to the backbone, A different ID was used, or the record names were used, as no ID lookup successfully linked to the backbone.",
  "enums.eventIssueDescription.TAXON_MATCH_TAXON_CONCEPT_ID_IGNORED": "taxonConceptID was not used when mapping the record to the GBIF backbone. This may indicate one of: The ID uses a pattern not configured for use by GBIF, The ID did not uniquely identify a concept in the checklist, The ID found a concept in the checklist that did not map to the backbone, A different ID was used, or the record names were used, as no ID lookup successfully linked to the backbone.",
  "enums.eventIssueDescription.TAXON_MATCH_TAXON_ID_IGNORED": "The taxonID was not used when mapping the record to the GBIF backbone. This may indicate one of: The ID uses a pattern not configured for use by GBIF, The ID did not uniquely identify a concept in the checklist, The ID found a concept in the checklist that did not map to the backbone, A different ID was used, or the record names were used, as no ID lookup successfully linked to the backbone.",
  "enums.eventIssueDescription.SCIENTIFIC_NAME_ID_NOT_FOUND": "The scientificNameID matched a known pattern, but it was not found in the associated checklist. The backbone lookup was performed using either the names or a different ID field from the record. This may indicate a poorly formatted identifier or may be caused by a newly created ID that isn't yet known in the version of the published checklist.",
  "enums.eventIssueDescription.TAXON_CONCEPT_ID_NOT_FOUND": "The taxonConceptID matched a known pattern, but it was not found in the associated checklist. The backbone lookup was performed using either the names or a different ID field from the record. This may indicate a poorly formatted identifier or may be caused by a newly created ID that isn't yet known in the version of the published checklist.",
  "enums.eventIssueDescription.TAXON_ID_NOT_FOUND": "The taxonID found matched a known pattern, but it was not found in the associated checklist. The backbone lookup was performed using either the names or a different ID field from the record. This may indicate a poorly formatted identifier or may be caused by a newly created ID that isn't yet known in the version of the published checklist.",
  "enums.eventIssueDescription.SCIENTIFIC_NAME_AND_ID_INCONSISTENT": "The scientificName provided in the occurrence record does not precisely match the name in the registered checklist when using the scientificNameID, taxonID or taxonConceptID to look it up. Publishers are advised to check the IDs are correct, or update the formatting of the names on their records.",
  "enums.eventIssueDescription.TAXON_MATCH_NAME_AND_ID_AMBIGUOUS": "The GBIF Backbone concept was found using the scientificNameID, taxonID or taxonConceptID, but it differs from what would have been found if the classification names on the record were used. This may indicate a gap in the GBIF backbone, a poor mapping between the checklist and the backbone, or a mismatch between the classification names and the declared IDs (scientificNameID or taxonConceptID) on the occurrence record itself.",
  "enums.eventIssueDescription.SUSPECTED_TYPE": "Coordinate accuracy invalid",
